text: A week passes. You go to classes. You eat lunch alone twice, then with Grubwin once, which was quiet but fine. He mostly read. You mostly read. You both pretended not to be grateful for the company.

On Wednesday, Vermia left a folded note in your shoe locker. It said: 'SCATHA WATCH: 3 signs this week!! 1) earthworm on north sidewalk 2) soil smell in hallway 3) YOU (still a sign)'. Signed with a small worm drawing.

On Thursday, Dirtus found you in the hall and showed you slide four of the Summoning Proposal without any preamble. Slide four was titled 'THE WORM MOON WINDOW: OPTIMAL DATES'. You didn't know what a worm moon was. He explained for eleven minutes.
It is now Friday. Second club meeting. You are, against all reasonable expectation, looking forward to it.

speaker: Grubwin
text: She's not here yet. She texted saying she has an 'important spiritual preparation' to do first. I've learned not to ask.

speaker: Mawla
text: She spent forty minutes organizing the sacred bookshelf by 'worm energy density'. I know because she called me about it.

speaker: Grubwin
text: At what time?

speaker: Mawla
text: Seven AM.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I'm sorry.

speaker: Mawla
text: It's fine. I was already awake. I don't sleep that well.
A small silence. There's something in how she said that — matter-of-fact, not fishing for sympathy. You file it away.

speaker: Vermia
text: FRIENDS. I have had a vision.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Good morning to you too.

speaker: Vermia
text: I was reorganizing the shelf — don't ask, Mawla knows — and I found a poem I wrote in year one. In the back of an old journal. And I think Scatha wanted me to find it today. For [player].

speaker: Vermia
text: Because it's about joining something you don't understand and finding it becomes part of you anyway. And that's— that's new member energy. That's the beginning energy.
She reads it aloud. It's short — six lines. Her voice is different when she reads her own poetry. Quieter. Like the performance switches off.

speaker: Vermia
text: 'Beneath the word, beneath the name / A worm turns slow, and turns the same / I did not know what I was seeking / Only that the ground was speaking / And so I pressed my ear to earth / And called what I found there — worth.'
Silence.

speaker: Mawla
text: That one's really good, Vermia.

speaker: Vermia
text: I know.

speaker: Vermia
text: I cried a little when I found it.

speaker: Grubwin
text: You cry at everything.

speaker: Vermia
text: That's because I feel things FULLY, Grubwin.
The door opens. Dirtus enters carrying a paper bag that clinks slightly.

speaker: Dirtus
text: I have acquired the ceremonial worm bait.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Why does it clink?

speaker: Dirtus
text: I put it in a jar. For dignity.

speaker: Mawla
text: You put worm bait in a jar for dignity.

speaker: Dirtus
text: A nice jar. From a candle. I cleaned it.
He holds it up. It is indeed a nice jar — amber glass, slightly fancy. You respect the commitment even as you question everything.

options.label: Ask Vermia about writing her first poem
Ask Dirtus when exactly he plans to do the summoning
Ask Grubwin how he ended up here
